title: The Complete Mediterranean Cookbook
author: America's Test Kitchen
cuisine: mediterranean (entire book) - a loved cuisine; strong, reliable source (ATK kitchen-tested)
note: Catalog built from chapter recipe-list pages (titles + page numbers). To add any as a real cookwhat recipe, scan the full recipe page for the ingredient list + method. PARTIAL: covers Rice & Grains (ch.4), Pasta & Couscous (ch.5), Vegetables (ch.7), Poultry/Pork/Lamb/Beef, Spice Blends & Pastes, Seafood (ch.8), Eggs (ch.10), Breads/Flatbreads/Pizzas (ch.11), and Fruit & Sweets (ch.12). Now also includes Meze/Antipasti/Small Plates (ch.1), Soups (ch.2), and Salads (ch.3) - essentially the full book. A 'Beans' chapter (if the book has one) may not be captured. A few lamb page numbers are best-effort.
source_format: Source for any meal cataloged from this book: "The Complete Mediterranean Cookbook (ATK) - p.NNN" (no URL).
dislikeFlags: Broccoli and Feta Frittata flagged (broccoli). White-bean/chickpea mains flagged (keep beans low). No curry dishes.

recipes.title: Sauteed Chicken Cutlets with Romesco Sauce
Sauteed Chicken Cutlets with Sun-Dried Tomato Sauce
Sauteed Chicken Breasts with Cherry Tomatoes, Zucchini, and Yellow Squash
Pan-Seared Chicken Breasts with Chickpea Salad

ingredients: 1 (14.5-oz) can whole peeled tomatoes, drained and chopped
captured.method: Brown the seasoned thighs; set aside. Soften onions, then add garlic, bay leaf, and cinnamon. Add wine and tomatoes, return the chicken, and braise in the oven until tender. Grind some toasted almonds to thicken the sauce; finish with parsley, lemon juice, and the remaining almonds.

Grilled Swordfish with Eggplant, Tomato, and Chickpea Salad

ingredients: 2 lb skinless swordfish steaks, cut into 1-inch pieces
1 (28-oz) can whole peeled tomatoes, drained (juice reserved) and chopped
captured.method: Mix pine nuts, mint, a quarter of the garlic, and orange zest for serving. Soften onion + celery, add the remaining garlic, thyme, and pepper flakes. Add wine + reserved tomato juice and reduce by half; add tomatoes, clam juice, raisins, and capers; simmer ~15 min. Nestle in the seasoned swordfish, simmer 4 min, then off heat covered 3 min. Serve sprinkled with the pine nut mixture.
